An Overview Of International Collaborative Health Research Projects Approved By Health Ministry

The Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR), New Delhi, is the apex body in India for the formulation, coordination and promotion of biomedical research. Established in 1911, it is one of the oldest medical research bodies in the world.
Cutting-edge research for evidence driven change
Chart a career at India's apex body of medical research

ICMR National Institute for Tribal Health Research (NITHR), Jabalpur

ICMR-NIHR conducts research on health issues that affect India’s tribal populations, including nutritional disorders, communicable and non-communicable diseases prevalent in tribal communities, and environmental health problems.
ICMR National Institute of Health Research (NIHR), Jodhpur

The National Institute for Health Research (NIHR), Jodhpur, was established on 27 June 1984 as the Desert Medicine Research Centre (DMRC). It was later renamed the National Institute for Implementation Research on Non-Communicable Diseases (NIIRNCD) on 7 December 2019 and subsequently redesignated as NIHR on 29 April 2026.

NIHR focuses on generating high-impact research across priority domains, strengthening capacity through specialized training programmes, and developing effective behaviour change communication strategies to address non-communicable disease (NCD) risk factors.
ICMR National Institute of Health Research, Bhubaneswar

ICMR-NIHR conducts interdisciplinary research on regionally prevailing communicable and non-communicable diseases, provides support in disease surveillance activities in the form of epidemic investigation and diagnosis, evaluation and training.
ICMR National Institute of Health Research, Gorakhpur
ICMR-NIHR is responsible for carrying out basic clinical, as well as operational research on locally prevalent diseases and suggesting intervention methods for reducing disease burden.
ICMR National Institute of Health Research, Dibrugarh

ICMR-NIHR conducts interdisciplinary research on prevailing communicable and non-communicable diseases in the North-East and provides support in disease surveillance activities in the form of epidemic investigation and diagnosis, evaluation and training.
ICMR Rajendra Memorial National Institute of Health Research, Patna
ICMR-RM NIHR conducts research on various aspects of Visceral Leishmaniasis (Kala-azar), HIV/AIDS, Tuberculosis, and other tropical diseases.
ICMR National Institute of Occupational Health Research, Ahmedabad

ICMR-NIOH conducts research and disseminates knowledge on occupational and environmental health with the aim to provide a safe, healthy and comfortable work environment to workers of all occupations. It has two Regional Centres at Kolkata and Bengaluru.
ICMR National Institute of NCDs Epidemiology, Bengaluru

ICMR-NINE works to develop and sustain national research databases on various diseases including cancer, diabetes, cardiovascular diseases and stroke, by leveraging recent advances in information technology.
ICMR Regional Occupational Health Centre (Southern) - NIOH

The ICMR Regional Occupational Health Centre (Southern), located in Bengaluru Karnataka, was established as a regional unit of ICMR-National Institute of Occupational Health (ICMR-NIOH) in 1977, to strengthen research on occupational and environmental health.
ICMR Bhopal Memorial Hospital & Research Center, Bhopal

The Bhopal Memorial Hospital and Research Centre, is a multi-specialty tertiary care centre, set up to provide medical facilities to all registered gas victims, and also carry out basic clinical and epidemiological research in all disciplines.
ICMR National Institute for Research in Environmental Health, Bhopal
ICMR-NIREH focuses on conducting biomedical research on environmental health issues and building capacity to address environmental health challenges.
ICMR National Institute of Child Health Research, Delhi

ICMR-NICHR (Formerly known as ICMR-NIP) conducts basic and applied research on various aspects of pathology (including histopathology, cytopathology and molecular pathology) to address health problems of national importance.
ICMR National Institute for Research in Digital Health, Delhi

ICMR-NIRDH (Formerly known as ICMR-NIMS) supports the development, application, and dissemination of statistical sciences in medicine through research, education, publications and advocacy. It also provides technical assistance to institutes across india.
ICMR National Institute of Malaria Research, Delhi
ICMR-NIMR conducts basic, applied and operational field research to find short-term and long-term solutions for malaria and other vector-borne diseases like dengue and chikungunya.
ICMR National Institute for Pre-Clinical Research, Hyderabad

ICMR-NIPCR pursues and supports basic and pre-clinical research on laboratory animals in the areas of developmental biology, reproductive biology, neurobiology, behavioral sciences, cardiology, stem cell, molecular cell biology, immunology and virology.
ICMR National Institute of Nutrition, Hyderabad
ICMR-NIN conducts research on nutrition and related fields to generate high-quality evidence for tackling important public health nutrition challenges.
ICMR National Institute for Research in Bacterial Infections, Kolkata
ICMR-NIRBI (Formerly known as ICMR-NICED) conducts basic and applied research on Cholera and other acute diarrhea diseases as well as on typhoid fever, infective hepatitis and HIV.
ICMR Centre for Ageing & Mental Health
The ICMR-Centre for Ageing and Mental Health (CAMH) does research on mental health and ageing associated health issues.
ICMR National Institute for Research on Blood and Immune Disorders, Mumbai

ICMR-NIBID conducts multidisciplinary research related to immunohaematology, offers diagnostic services, and provides training to medical and non-medical professionals in the field of hematology, immunology, and genetics.
ICMR National Institute for Research on Women's Health, Mumbai
ICMR-NIWHR works to improve women’s reproductive health as well as address child health issues through research, education, and healthcare services.
ICMR National Institute of Vector Control Research, Puducherry
ICMR-NIVCR is engaged in basic and applied research with the primary objective of finding newer methods and developing strategies for the control of vector borne diseases.
ICMR National Institute of Translational Virology and AIDS Research, Pune

ICMR-NITVAR (Formerly known as ICMR-NARI) conducts research on HIV prevention and care, and supports the National AIDS Control Programme, in the areas of surveillance, capacity-building, laboratory services and drug resistance studies.
ICMR National Institute of Virology, Pune

ICMR-NIV undertakes comprehensive research on viruses of public health concern, helps conduct outbreak investigations and monitor the spread of viral diseases, and contributes to the development of vaccines.
ICMR National Institute of One Health, Nagpur
ICMR National JALMA Institute for Leprosy & Other Mycobacterial Diseases, Agra
ICMR-NJILOMD conducts research on leprosy, tuberculosis and other mycobacterial diseases, HIV, and has also commenced research on other diseases such as filariasis.
ICMR National Institute of Traditional Medicine, Belagavi
ICMR-NITM works to facilitate the integration of traditional medicinal practices with modern health systems through research, human resource development, and outreach activities.
ICMR National Institute for Research in Tuberculosis, Chennai
ICMR-NIRT carries out research on all aspects (clinical, bacteriological, epidemiological, behavioural and laboratory) of tuberculosis and HIV-TB, with a focus on treatment, prevention, and control.
ICMR National Institute of Epidemiology, Chennai

ICMR-NIE conducts epidemiological studies, helps develop human resources in epidemiology and biostatistics, and coordinates between various ICMR and non-ICMR Institutes at the national level for epidemiological purposes.
ICMR National Institute of Cancer Prevention and Research, Noida

ICMR-NICPR conducts basic, clinical, and applied research related to the common cancers that are prevalent in India, involving medical and community practice with an emphasis on early detection and prevention.
ICMR Regional Medical Research Centre, Sri Vijaya Puram

ICMR-RMRCSVP carries out biomedical research on locally prevalent communicable and noncommunicable diseases with a special emphasis on the health problems of indigenous tribal population of the region.
Medical Device and Diagnostics Mission Secretariat (MDMS)
Indian Clinical Trial and Education Network (INTENT)
